title: calvoa grandifolia
intro: Calvoa grandifolia is a plant species native to tropical Africa, belonging to the family Calophyllaceae and is commonly known as the African bush mango. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat various health conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections due to its antiseptic, antifungal, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Calvoa grandifolia include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function, making it a valuable asset in traditional medicine. Herbal preparations of Calvoa grandifolia can be made by infusing the leaves in hot water to create a tea or by using the bark to make a decoction, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
